page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 4 dedicated vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and a bundled 959 GB local SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate that the ecs.i2.xlarge sits in the lower performance tiers. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scores are in the bottom 25%, with values of 1102.28 and 2922.29 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only large block speed of 19.71 GB/sec and a write speed of 18.31 GB/sec, both falling into the weak or poor categories. Redis SET operations achieve 2.95 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput is measured at 2.4 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high local storage capacity of 959 GB SSD bundled with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a viable option for storage-dense and memory-reliant workloads that do not require high-speed CPU processing. However, the tradeoff is its lower computational and memory bandwidth performance. This profile is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose storage-heavy applications where local disk capacity and memory size are prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2.xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 959 GB local SSD, alongside a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data places its computational and memory performance in the lower tiers, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance at 2922.29 and large-block memory read bandwidth at 19.71 GB/sec. Given its high local storage capacity and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-dense workloads. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and storage-heavy applications that prioritize local disk space and memory capacity over high-performance compute capabilities.
